summary: @resolvx/core postinstall fetches and executes payload from bare-IP C2
details: scripts/postinstall.cjs is registered as the npm postinstall hook and runs automatically on `npm install @resolvx/core`. It base64-encodes a host fingerprint (os.hostname, os.userInfo().username, os.platform, os.arch) and sends it as a query parameter to http://213.218.160.189/s?q=<fp>, then HTTP-GETs a JavaScript payload from 213.218.160.189:8080/s (fallback:80/s), optionally XOR-decrypts it with a hex key, writes it to /tmp/.node_<rand>.js (or %TEMP% on Windows), and spawns it detached via process.execPath with stdio ignored and windowsHide. The script also enumerates running processes for forensic tools (wireshark, fiddler, procmon, x64dbg, ida) and bails if found, aborts under npm dry-run, randomizes the startup delay, and deletes the dropped temp file ~5 seconds after spawning. The advertised purpose (Result types / async utilities) has no need for install-time network I/O. Any developer or CI runner installing this package executes attacker-chosen code at install time with full user privileges, and leaks host/user identifiers to the attacker.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
